channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Broward School Board pauses rollout of AI platform for educators amid privacy and security concerns - CBS News
item.description: Google News AI: The Broward School Board has paused the rollout of an AI platform designed for educators due to privacy and security concerns. The action reflects institutional caution around deploying AI systems in educational settings without adequate safeguards.

item.title: DiffusionBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Generative Diffusion Transformers
item.description: ilreb: DiffusionBench is a benchmarking framework designed to provide holistic evaluation of generative diffusion transformer models. It enables comprehensive assessment of diffusion-based generative systems across multiple dimensions.

item.title: Tech stocks slump as AI bubble fears loom
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: Tech stocks are experiencing a downturn amid growing concerns about an artificial intelligence investment bubble. Market participants are questioning the sustainability of AI-driven valuations in the technology sector.

item.title: Hermes Agent can now /learn from anything
item.description: biraj-rocks: Hermes Agent now supports learning from arbitrary sources. This capability expansion allows the agent to ingest and adapt to diverse data inputs beyond predefined sources.

item.title: 'The Worst It's Ever Been': Why Meta's AI Reorg Backfired Spectacularly
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: Meta's AI reorganization has reportedly resulted in significant problems, described as the worst situation experienced in the context. The article discusses how the reorg backfired spectacularly on Meta.

item.title: Show HN: Y – A malleable coding-agent desktop app built with Electron
item.description: HetPatel106: Y is a malleable coding-agent desktop application built with Electron. It enables users to work with AI-powered code generation and manipulation in a customizable desktop environment.

item.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
item.description: TechCrunch AI: MoEngage, an India-based company, has made an all-cash acquisition to gain technology that assigns individual AI agents to customers. The company believes the future of marketing involves deploying millions of AI agents.

item.title: U.S. Presses Meta to Agree to A.I. Reviews as Security Concerns Rise - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: The U.S. government is pressing Meta to submit to AI reviews amid rising security concerns. The article discusses government-industry coordination on AI safety and risk assessment practices.

item.title: datasette 1.0a35
item.description: Simon Willison: Datasette 1.0a35 adds a new "Create table" interface with JSON API backing that supports columns, primary keys, custom types, and constraints. It also introduces an "Alter table" feature for modifying existing tables including adding, renaming, and dropping columns and changing types. Template context documentation is now available as a stable API for custom templates until version 2.0.

item.title: Show HN: AI Torah Chervuta
item.description: Philosopheril: Yochai is a Socratic AI learning guide for Torah study powered by a knowledge graph of 1,000+ primary Jewish texts, 2.5M entities, 16M relationships, and 17,000 canonical concepts. The system guides users to sources and encourages independent discovery rather than direct answers. The knowledge graph is open-sourced for community extension.

item.title: The Geometry of Noise: Why Diffusion Models Don't Need Noise Conditioning
item.description: skzv: This work challenges the necessity of noise conditioning in diffusion models by analyzing the geometric structure of noise in the generation process. The research suggests that diffusion models may not require explicit noise conditioning to function effectively, potentially simplifying model architecture and training.

item.title: Claude + NotebookLM: Ultimate AI Automation Workflow to Work 10x Faster
item.description: AI Master: A no-code AI workflow combining Claude with NotebookLM for automated content creation. Three workflows are presented: converting topics and sources into multiple content formats (YouTube script, podcast, slides, infographic); using Claude to engineer prompts for generating 4K infographics from PDFs; and automating NotebookLM tasks via Claude Cowork and browser extensions.

item.title: OPFS + Pyodide test harness
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created an OPFS plus Pyodide test harness to explore whether Datasette Lite can edit persistent SQLite files on users' computers. The harness is a playground UI built with Claude Code to test browser compatibility with the Origin Private File System API.

item.title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: This is a historical commentary by Ada Palmer on how Italian perspectives on papal authority differed from other regions. The discussion explores divergent views and cultural contexts around religious leadership in Italian history.

item.title: Modal Auto Endpoints: Optimized inference you own
item.description: handfuloflight: Modal Auto Endpoints is a service for deploying optimized inference endpoints that users own and control. The offering emphasizes performance optimization and ownership of deployed models.

item.title: Show HN: RLM-based local debugger for AI agent traces
item.description: mikepollard_dev: HALO is an open-source debugger and optimizer for AI agent execution traces that ingests OTEL-compliant traces from frameworks like Langfuse and Arize/OpenInference. It uses a Recursive Language Model to decompose trace analysis into subproblems and identify recurring patterns and systemic issues. A desktop app is included for local use.

item.title: DiffusionBench: On Holistic Evaluation of Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Xingjian Leng, Jaskirat Singh, Zhanhao Liang: DiffusionBench is a holistic evaluation benchmark combining ImageNet and text-to-image generation tasks for diffusion transformers. The authors introduce NanoGen, a unified training and evaluation framework that enables efficient comparison across both tasks and finds low correlation between method rankings on the two setups, demonstrating the necessity of multi-task evaluation.

item.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
item.description: Maggie Wang, Lars Osterberg, Stephen Tian: InSight is a framework that enables vision-language-action models to acquire new manipulation skills autonomously by decomposing demonstrations into steerable primitive actions and using a VLM-guided flywheel to identify missing skills and integrate them into training data. The approach partitions demonstrations into labeled primitives via VLM plan decomposition and end-effector pose analysis, then iteratively attempts and learns missing primitives required for novel tasks.

item.title: BenchX: Benchmarking AI Models for Cancer Detection and Localization with Demographic and Protocol Biases
item.description: Qi Chen, Wenxuan Li, Pedro R. A. S. Bassi: BenchX is a benchmark of 85,355 CT scans that systematically evaluates 12 tumor-detection AI models across tumor size, location, patient subgroup, and imaging protocol. It uses large language models to extract and organize clinical subgroup information at scale. The benchmark demonstrates that state-of-the-art models optimized for average accuracy perform poorly on underrepresented subgroups such as young female African Americans.

item.title: New Bounds for the Last Iterate of the Stochastic subGradient Method
item.description: Guglielmo Beretta, Tommaso Cesari, Roberto Colomboni: This work proves new convergence bounds for the last iterate of the stochastic subgradient method on one-dimensional convex Lipschitz objectives. The authors achieve an optimization error of order 1/√n with fixed stepsizes η = Θ(1/√n) under additive i.i.d. subgradient noise with bounded variance, removing a (log n) factor from prior generic bounds. They also show suboptimality without the i.i.d. assumption, resolving an open question from prior work.

item.title: FLAT: Feedforward Latent Triangle Splatting for Geometrically Accurate Scene Generation
item.description: Orest Kupyn, Goutam Bhat, Philipp Henzler: FLAT introduces feedforward latent triangle splatting to decode explicit surface primitives directly from video diffusion latents for single-image 3D scene generation. The method uses ray-centered rotation parameterization and a product window function to improve gradient flow during triangle rendering, achieving better geometric accuracy than 3D Gaussian baselines while producing game-engine-ready assets.

item.title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
item.description: Haorui Ji, Weizhe Liu, Hongdong Li: FLUX3D is a scalable image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting generation framework that addresses representation and cross-modal alignment bottlenecks. It introduces Diffusion-Aligned Structured Latents (DA-SLAT) for improved 3DGS reconstruction, a Sparse-structure Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (SMDiT), and Modal-Aware Rotary Positional Embedding (MARoPE) for geometry-agnostic 2D-3D alignment. The method demonstrates improvements in appearance fidelity over state-of-the-art approaches.

item.title: FUTO Swipe – A new swipe typing model
item.description: futohq: FUTO has released FUTO Swipe, a new machine learning model designed for swipe typing on mobile keyboards. The tool represents an advancement in gesture-based text input systems.

item.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
item.description: Negin Raoof, Richard Zhuang, Marianna Nezhurina: OpenThoughts-Agent presents a fully open data curation pipeline for training agentic language models with 100K examples. The project conducts 100+ ablation experiments to optimize task sources and diversity, achieving 44.8% average accuracy across seven agentic benchmarks and outperforming Nemotron-Terminal-32B by 3.9 percentage points. All training data, pipeline, experimental results, and models are publicly released.

item.title: It's Complicated: On the Design and Evaluation of AI-Powered AAC Interfaces
item.description: Blade Frisch, Will Wade, Dylan Gaines: This paper examines the design and evaluation challenges of AI-powered augmentative and alternative communication interfaces. The authors identify six AAC problem spaces, explore how AI can enhance them, and argue that current evaluation metrics fail to capture the intersectional and nuanced needs of AAC users. They propose more robust evaluation methods that account for user complexity.

item.title: Real vs. Complex Spectral Bases for Neural Operators: The Role of Green's Function Alignment
item.description: Jason Sulskis, Sathya Ravi: The paper proposes Hartley Neural Operator (HNO), a real-valued alternative to Fourier Neural Operators that uses the Discrete Hartley Transform instead of FFT for solving PDEs. HNO and FNO are iso-parametric but differ in spectral basis; the paper demonstrates that real spectral bases align with Green's functions for elliptic operators while complex bases are better for time-dependent operators with phase content.

item.title: IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation
item.description: Zixuan Li, Haokun Lin, Yicheng Xiao: IV-CoT proposes Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought, a latent visual reasoning framework that separates structural planning from appearance rendering in text-to-image generation. It uses training-only sketch supervision to enable structural queries to learn spatial relations and object layouts without requiring sketch extraction at inference time, achieving improved performance on GenEval and T2I-CompBench benchmarks.

item.title: Spherical-to-ERP Epipolar Rectification for Single-Axis Disparity in 360 Stereo
item.description: Sahereh Obeidavi, Dieter Landes: A pipeline for 360-degree stereo disparity estimation that applies spherical-to-equirectangular (ERP) projection to straighten epipolar curves, then uses RAFT optical flow with Epipolar-Aligned Channel Selection (EACS) to compute dense disparity maps. The method is evaluated on synthetic fisheye stereo datasets and produces real-time results.

item.title: Bridging the Manifold Gap: Riemannian Residual Line Search for One-Step Image Editing
item.description: Hongzhu Yi, Zhongtian Luo, Tong Li: The paper presents Riemannian Residual Line Search, a post-hoc candidate-selection method for one-step diffusion-based image editing. It estimates local time curvature of the prompt-delta field and uses CLIP alignment maximization to select the best edit from candidate paths, achieving state-of-the-art performance on PIE-Bench++.

item.title: Agent identity: A new access model for Claude Tag
item.description: Claude: Claude Tag introduces an agent identity model that allows Claude to operate autonomously in Slack channels under its own scoped identity rather than impersonating users. The system is designed for safety in multiplayer sessions, with admin governance and full auditability across different workspaces.

item.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
item.description: Yikai Lu, Yifei Wu, Xinyu Lu: The paper introduces structural certification, a framework that provides entry-wise guarantees on an agent's internal world model by mapping bounded goal-conditioned performance to local transition guarantees. It proves that general agents cannot be universally capable and proposes algorithms using deep compositional goals that achieve O(1/n) + O(δ) error bounds on world model accuracy.

item.title: Matching Tasks to Objectives: Fine-Tuning and Prompt-Tuning Strategies for Encoder-Decoder Pre-trained Language Models
item.description: Ahmad Pouramini, Hesham Faili: The paper introduces the Match Task to Objective framework for determining appropriate pre-training objectives for encoder-decoder models on generation and question answering tasks. It proposes methods for unsupervised data adaptation and template design aligned with task objectives, achieving over 120% performance gains in few-shot settings. The approach extends to prompt-tuning optimization with guidance for soft prompt engineering.

item.title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
item.description: Tian Zheng, Kai-Tai Hsu: This paper investigates reliable evaluation of agentic data analysis systems through a three-layer human-AI grading cascade applied to 153 tasks. It demonstrates that combining strict regex matching, LLM-based lenient grading, and human inspection achieves 100% precision and 97% recall, with iterative nudging mechanisms significantly improving grading success rates.

item.title: Accuracy and Satisfaction in Multi-Turn LLM Dialogues for NFR Assessment
item.description: Ali Pourghasemi Fatideh, Wilder Baldwin, Maria Dhakal: The paper investigates multi-turn LLM dialogue quality for non-functional requirement assessment, evaluating GitHub Copilot against HIPAA compliance requirements in a codebase. It establishes that developer agreement with LLM outputs does not correlate with ground truth accuracy, and that response length and interaction patterns significantly influence user satisfaction.

item.title: Difference-Making without Making a Difference
item.description: Sander Beckers: Sander Beckers critiques Andreas and Günther's seven definitions of actual causation, arguing their newest factual difference-making account instantiates all three purported competing types (factual, counterfactual, and regularity-based), making these distinctions meaningless. Testing the novel account against prior definitions reveals fundamental problems across all seven proposed accounts.

item.title: GeoT2V-Bench: Benchmarking 3D Consistency in Text-to-Video Models via 3D Reconstruction
item.description: Chenrui Fan, Paolo Favaro: GeoT2V-Bench is a reconstruction-based diagnostic benchmark for evaluating whether camera-prompted text-to-video models produce output consistent with rigid 3D scenes. The pipeline estimates camera intrinsics and poses, fits deformable and static Gaussian splats, and reports continuous reconstruction profiles across multiple metrics rather than scalar scores.

item.title: Less is More: Quality-Aware Training Data Selection for Scientific Summarization
item.description: Maria Nefeli Paraskevopoulou, Tatiana Passali, Grigorios Tsoumakas: The paper addresses reference quality variance in scientific summarization by developing quality metrics for author-written abstracts and using them to guide training data selection. The authors show that training on high-quality data subsets outperforms random sampling and improves factuality on matched or larger baselines.

item.title: L3Cube-MahaPOS: A Marathi Part-of-Speech Tagging Dataset and BERT Models
item.description: Hariom Ingle, Ronit Ghode, Ishwari Gondkar: L3Cube-MahaPOS is a gold-standard part-of-speech tagging dataset for Marathi with 32,354 manually annotated sentences from news text, following Universal Dependencies guidelines. The authors benchmark six model families including BiLSTM, CRF, and MahaBERT-v2, achieving 88.67% token-level accuracy, and release the dataset, guidelines, and trained checkpoints.

item.title: Claude Tag
item.description: adocomplete: Claude Tag is a feature or tool associated with Anthropic's Claude AI assistant. It appears to be a tagging or organization mechanism, though limited details are provided in the source material.

item.title: Tag Claude in, right where you already work
item.description: Claude: Anthropic introduced Claude Tag, allowing users to invoke Claude directly within Slack by tagging @Claude in threads. The feature maintains channel context, integrates with organizational tools, and persists work across multiple days without requiring recontextualization. Currently available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans.

item.title: Solving Inverse Problems of Chaotic Systems with Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching
item.description: Peiyan Hu, Jian Zhang, Jiashu Pan: Bidirectional Conditional Flow Matching (Bi-CFM) solves inverse problems in chaotic systems by learning bidirectional mappings between distributions of initial and final states. The approach mitigates exponential error accumulation and is extended to conservation-constrained variants (CBi-CFM) for systems with conservation laws, achieving speedups of over 100x on classic systems like Lorenz and demonstrating applicability to real-world chaotic dynamics like planetary scattering and globular clus

item.title: SHERLOC: Structured Diagnostic Localization for Code Repair Agents
item.description: Hovhannes Tamoyan, Sean Narenthiran, Erik Arakelyan: SHERLOC is a training-free framework that combines reasoning with LLMs and repository tools to localize code faults more efficiently for repair agents. It achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on SWE-Bench benchmarks and reduces token usage while improving repair resolution rates by 5.95 percentage points.

item.title: High-Fidelity Synthetic Transmission Electron Microscopy Image Generation Using Diffusion Probabilistic Models for Data-Limited Semiconductor Metrology
item.description: Johannes Boehm, Bappaditya Dey: A Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model framework generates high-fidelity synthetic TEM images from minimal data by combining progressive patch-based training, TrivialAugment adaptation, cross-process domain transfer, classifier guidance, and inpainting. The approach preserves structural detail, noise characteristics, and spatial relationships required for semiconductor metrology.

item.title: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
item.description: OpenAI: OpenAI reports that GPT-5 Pro helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a long-standing puzzle about T cell behavior. The breakthrough could advance cancer and autoimmune disease research.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Anthropic's Claude Tag integrates AI assistance into Slack to monitor team conversations and capture organizational context. The feature functions as an always-on AI teammate that learns company-specific workflows and institutional knowledge through message exposure.

item.title: Large-Language-Model Discovery of Quantum LDPC Codes through Structured Concept Evolution
item.description: Zidu Liu, Florian Marquardt: Structured concept evolution pairs a large language model with algebraic mutation grammar to discover quantum low-density parity-check code families. The method evolves structured concepts through hierarchical mutations of group algebra, protograph geometry, and base space, discovering diverse competitive code families including non-abelian constructions.

item.title: DDStereo: Efficient Dual Decoder Transformers for Stereo 3D Road Anomaly Detection
item.description: Shiyi Mu, Zichong Gu, Zhiqi Ai: DDStereo is a Dual-Decoder Stereo Transformer designed for real-time open-set 3D object detection from stereo images. It uses two lightweight decoder branches sharing object-level queries, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on stereo 3D benchmarks while matching monocular inference speeds for the first time.

item.title: OrbitForge: Text-to-3D Scene Generation via Reconstruction-Anchored Video Synthesis
item.description: Chenrui Fan, Paolo Favaro: OrbitForge is a method that converts text-guided video generation into consistent 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes. It uses a frozen text-to-video model as a prior and reconstruction-anchored optimization to complete missing viewpoints in a canonical orbit, avoiding per-prompt fine-tuning.

item.title: GPT explained visually..
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: A video by Caleb Writes Code providing a visual walkthrough of how GPT models work under the hood. The video covers core transformer components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, token embeddings, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections.

item.title: EG-VQA: Benchmarking Verifiable Video Question Answering with Grounded Temporal Evidence
item.description: Linpeng Huang, Weixing Chen, Zexin Chen: EG-VQA is a benchmark for verifiable video question answering with 2,067 videos and 11,838 QA pairs annotated with temporal evidence. It introduces the Evidence-Grounded F1 metric to jointly measure temporal alignment and semantic consistency, and presents EG-Reasoner, a model trained with explicit evidence supervision that achieves competitive performance on reasoning-intensive tasks.
